title: Modi, Shah are traitors who are attacking the Constitution, says Rahul at Rae Bareli meet
content: Congress leader says that the BJP government is trying to do away with reservation given for marginalised sections

title: No double standards, we condemn Russia, U.S. and Israel for international law violations: Norway’s Foreign Minister
content: In an interview after Modi-Store bilateral, Norwegian FM Eide says India and Norway must resist over-dependence on global powers for trade or technology

title: PM busy handing out candies in Italy amid economic storm at home, says Congress
content: Rahul Gandhi called the reel that shows Prime Minister Narendra Modi handing over a packet of toffees to Italy’s Giorgia Meloni “an insult to the people of the country”

title: PIL plea filed against deposit of temple funds in State-owned non banking finance corporations
content: Justices G.R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan admit the plea and insist on filing of counter affidavit within a week

title: Karnataka draws up plan to supply 2.5 tmcft of Cauvery water from Kollegal to Bengaluru’s industrial areas
content: Large and Medium Industries Minister M.B. Patil says the Cauvery Water Disputes Tribunal had allocated 4 tmcft of water for industrial purposes; of this, only 1.37 tmcft is being utilised

title: Forest-dwelling tribes push for direct recruitment
content: The government had announced that 12 tribes residing on the forest fringes would be given jobs in the civil services through a special direct recruitment

title: Slew of road reforms planned for ULBs in A.P., standardised road lane markings made mandatory
content: All ULBs have been told to ensure edge lines, stop lines and zebra crossings, black-and-yellow kerb painting on roads, and cautionary traffic signage and rectification of minor road defects

title: Twisha Sharma death: NCW seeks action taken report from Madhya Pradesh government
content: Details sought regarding FIR provisions, status of accused persons’ arrest and interrogation, efforts to arrest absconding husband, passport impoundment status, CCTV footage, call records, electronic and forensic evidence, post-mortem report and action taken on any prior complaints

title: Patient injured after ceiling portion collapses at Guntur GGH
content: Preliminary assessment suggests that water leakage and rat infestation had damaged the ceiling sheet, leading to its collapse, says GGH Superintendent

title: CPI seeks legal guidelines for regulating online medicine sales
content: CPI demands that the government strengthen Drug Control Administration and State Pharmacy Council, introduce strict digital monitoring systems and allow qualified pharmacists to set up independent community pharmacy consultation clinics

title: CPI(M) opposes population incentive policy of A.P. government
content: Encouraging larger families without improving living standards will push the society into poverty and inequality, says CPI(M) State secretary

title: Supreme Court inclined to grant bail to 2020 Delhi riots accused Tasleem Ahmed and Abdul Khalid Saifi
content: Court says it is prima facie inclined to grant bail to 2020 Delhi riots accused Tasleem Ahmed and Abdul Khalid Saifi; ASG argues that conflicting views on bail in UAPA cases need to be settled by a larger Bench

title: Centre tells Supreme Court no new hydel projects should come up in upper Ganga
content: Three Union Ministries submit common affidavit in top court saying the government is not in favour of permitting any new projects in the Alaknanda and Bhagirathi river basins in Uttarakhand, apart from the seven already commissioned

title: Welfare and safety of workers top on govt. agenda, asserts Labour Minister Subhash
content: The NDA government has restored the schemes discontinued by the previous YSRCP dispensation, while creating an industry-friendly atmosphere, he says

title: Eviction attempt triggers high tension at Dalit settlement in Kunnathunadu
content: The move follows a Supreme Court verdict four years ago in favour of a private individual in a land dispute involving 2.65 acres allegedly falling within 19.30 acres of puramboke land

title: Congress set to join Tamil Nadu’s first coalition government on May 21
content: “We invite VCK leader Thol. Thirumavalavan to be part of the Council of Ministers. This is the wish of the Chief Minister as well. Similarly, the IUML should also join the Council of Ministers,” Minister Aadhav Arjuna told reporters in Chennai.

title: Telangana heatwave | CM urges people to take adequate precautions
content: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy directs officials to ensure availability of adequate ORS packets, supply of drinking water and uninterrupted power supply to residents

title: Bidadi township: 80% landowners agree to part with land, says DKS
content: The government is set to acquire over 6,700 acres for developing India’s first and largest artificial intelligence (AI)-powered integrated township

title: Naidu highlights importance of spirituality for holistic progress
content: Participating in the 70th birthday of Sri Sri Ravi Shankar, says the spiritual leader has earned the trust of both common people and global institutions while carrying India’s spiritual heritage across continents

title: NIA arrests Kolkata resident on espionage charge
content: Zafar Riaz alias Rizvi was allegedly involved in passing confidential security-related information to Pakistan intelligence officers

title: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio to start first India visit on May 23
content: Meeting comes amidst U.S.-Iran war that has hurt India’s energy security; trade and defence cooperation also on the agenda; U.S. Embassy notes that Quad ministerial meetings also expected soon

title: India and Italy have been in continuous contact over Ukraine and West Asia conflicts: PM Modi
content: Modi describes India-Italy relation as a ‘win-win partnership’ and welcomes the upgradation of bilateral ties to the level of ‘Special Strategic Partnership’

title: SRMIST, The Hindu to organise webinar on careers in agritech
content: Part of the ‘Future Career Conversations’ series, the discussion will explore the growing role of engineering in improving productivity, sustainability, and efficiency in farming practices

title: Rahul Gandhi’s ‘traitor’ remark on PM Modi reflects his ‘anarchic mindset’: BJP chief
content: Congress leader ‘will definitely pay a heavy price for such an unfortunate remark’, says Nabin in a video message

title: Rules for new rural employment scheme to be issued soon
content: The Ministry of Rural Development has informed the Parliamentary Committee that 25 States have allocated funds for the programme, and administrative and policy steps are being put in place

title: Watch: Why WHO is worried about Ebola outbreak | Above the Fold | 20.05.2026
content: In today’s episode, we look at the WHO’s highest-level alert over the Ebola outbreak in Central Africa, PM Modi’s meetings with Giorgia Meloni, Vladimir Putin’s high-stakes Beijing visit, the Supreme Court’s remarks backing caste enumeration in Census 2027, and Kerala’s decision on K-Rail SilverLine project and more.

title: Donald Trump warns Iran of ‘nasty’ US action as negotiations enter 'final stages'
content: President Trump announced the US is in the "final stages" of Iran negotiations, warning of "nasty" actions if a deal isn't reached.  He contrasted current US military engagements with past prolonged conflicts, highlighting reduced casualties. Trump also expressed strong support for Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, even joking about running for the position himself.

title: Centre ropes in Meta, Google, Telegram to curb fake NEET leak posts ahead of re-exam
content: Pradhan orders crackdown on misinformation networks; security and intelligence agencies asked to ensure foolproof conduct

title: 'Unfortunately, your role has been eliminated': Meta leadership in email to employees
content: Meta has initiated significant layoffs, impacting thousands globally as roles are eliminated to enhance efficiency and fund AI investments. Employees are informed via email about severance, visa support, and system access termination. A non-working notice period is in effect, with continued pay and benefits until the termination date. Outplacement services are also provided.

title: IPL: KKR beat MI to stay alive as playoff race heads into final round
content: Kolkata Knight Riders defeated Mumbai Indians by four wickets in a rain-affected IPL 2026 clash to keep their playoff hopes alive. MI managed only 147/8 after another poor batting display. Manish Pandey scored 45 and Rovman Powell made 40 as KKR chased down the target in 18.5 overs, with Rinku Singh hitting the winning runs.

title: After job cuts, Anthropic CEO now makes 'doomsday' prediction for software companies
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei told WSJ at the World Economic Forum in Davos that software is heading toward being "cheap, maybe essentially free," warning that whole careers built around writing code may not survive the AI shift. The Anthropic chief executive also predicted SaaS incumbents leaning on code complexity as a moat could "go bust" as ServiceNow, Snowflake and Microsoft stocks slide and Anthropic eyes a $900 billion valuation.

title: 'We've been quite poor': Hardik Pandya's blunt verdict on Mumbai Indians' IPL campaign
content: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya expressed frustration over his team's poor fielding, a key factor in their loss to Kolkata Knight Riders.  Despite opportunities, dropped catches proved costly. Pandya also felt the batting lineup was about 20 runs short of a competitive total. KKR's win keeps their playoff hopes alive.

title: Bryson DeChambeau slammed after moon landing claims, PGA flop
content: Bryson DeChambeau went viral after questioning the Apollo 11 moon landing footage during a podcast appearance. The comments came after he missed the cut at the PGA Championship. Fans heavily criticized his opinion online, while DeChambeau also opened up about possibly choosing YouTube content creation over professional golf in the future.

title: Russian warplanes ‘repeatedly’ intercepted RAF spy plane over Black Sea
content: Russian fighter jets "repeatedly and dangerously" intercepted an unarmed RAF spy plane over the Black Sea, disabling its autopilot and coming within metres of its nose. The Ministry of Defence condemned the "unacceptable behaviour" as the most dangerous encounter since 2022, risking accidents and escalation.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott can’t find place in top donors list despite ‘gifting’ billions
content: Despite gifting $7.1 billion in 2025, MacKenzie Scott was excluded from the Chronicle of Philanthropy's top donor list. Her substantial donations, exceeding Jeff Bezos' lifetime giving, were overlooked due to a lack of public details about Yield Giving's operations. Scott prioritizes private, impactful giving over public recognition.

title: Who is Tejasvi Singh Dahiya? KKR's Rs 3 crore concussion substitute vs Mumbai Indians
content: Tejasvi Singh Dahiya entered the IPL 2026 spotlight as a concussion substitute for Angkrish Raghuvanshi during a Kolkata Knight Riders match. The 23-year-old wicketkeeper-batter from Delhi was acquired by KKR for Rs 3 crore after a stellar Delhi Premier League campaign, marking a significant turnaround from being unsold in the previous auction.

title: Ronda Rousey’s net worth 2026: Earnings, endorsements and lifestyle
content: Ronda Rousey, a trailblazer in combat sports, is projected to have a net worth of nearly $14 million by 2026. Her fortune stems from lucrative UFC and WWE careers, acting, endorsements, and book sales. Rousey's impact on women's MMA and her transition to WWE cemented her status as a crossover star, ensuring her continued relevance and earning potential.

title: NHL Trade Rumors: Oilers, Panthers battle for $36M Team Canada star
content: The Edmonton Oilers are connected to Jordan Binnington as they reshape their goaltending ahead of a critical offseason. Binnington, coming off a poor regular season with the Blues, is available with one year left on his deal. But the Florida Panthers could complicate Edmonton's pursuit depending on Bobrovsky's contract situation. The Oilers also carry the weight of the failed Tristan Jarry trade, which adds salary cap complications that Bowman must resolve before making any significant move in goal.

title: Jeff Bezos favored to buy Seahawks from Microsoft co-founder’s estate
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos is projected as the leading buyer for the Seattle Seahawks. The NFL team was owned by late former Microsoft co-founder Paul Allen. After his passing in 2018, it is now managed by her sister Jody Allen under the Paul G. Allen Trust. It is now officially up for sale. Jeff Bezos has shown interest in owning a local NFL franchise for over seven years. The sale process is expected to conclude by the 2026 offseason.

title: Delhi, UP, Rajasthan, Punjab & more states sizzle in heatwave: What's fuelling temperature spike
content: India is grappling with an intense heatwave as temperatures soar, with the IMD issuing orange and red alerts for several northern and central regions. Delhi is experiencing highs of 44-45°C, while Uttar Pradesh's Banda district has recorded over 48°C. The extreme heat is also impacting tourism and is linked to global climate factors like potential El Niño conditions.

title: 42 aircraft, over $29 billion: What Trump’s Iran operation cost the US military
content: Operation Epic Fury, initiated under the leadership of Donald Trump, saw the U.S. military suffer grave losses, with 42 aircraft—comprising fighter jets and drones—sustaining damage or being lost entirely. This aggressive strike prompted swift and vigorous reprisal from Iran, bringing the region to a boiling point.

title: No Ebola cases reported in India yet; govt ramps up surveillance after WHO alert
content: India has reported no Ebola cases, despite the WHO declaring it a public health emergency. The health ministry has strengthened nationwide surveillance and preparedness measures, including sharing detailed SOPs with states and Union Territories. The country has prior experience in handling similar outbreaks.

title: Deepak Chahar mocks viral 'chit celebration' with hilarious no-chit act
content: Deepak Chahar humorously mimicked the viral 'chit celebration' trend during MI vs KKR, pretending to pull out an imaginary note after taking a wicket, much to his teammates' amusement. This trend, started by Abhishek Sharma, has seen mixed reactions, with some former cricketers criticizing it as 'rubbish'.

title: 'Do we finish the job?' Donald Trump loses patience over Iran negotiations deadlock
content: US President Donald Trump has signaled openness to renewed peace talks with Iran, a shift from his previous threats of military action. Saudi Arabia welcomed this diplomatic approach. Iran's chief negotiator, however, warned of retaliation, viewing the US stance as an attempt to reignite conflict.

title: PM Modi conferred Food and Agricultural Organisation's highest honour, Agricola Medal
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi received the FAO Agricola Medal, the UN's highest agriculture honour, for his work on food security and sustainable farming.  He dedicated the award to India's farmers and agricultural community, emphasizing the nation's shift towards 'Producing Better' with a focus on biodiversity and technology. Modi highlighted India's commitment to human welfare and global responsibility.

title: After 14 years, Mumbai Indians' Tilak Varma scripts rare 'unwanted' IPL record
content: Mumbai Indians faced Kolkata Knight Riders with no playoff pressure. Their batting collapsed, failing to post a competitive total. Tilak Varma set a 14-year-old IPL record for a slow strike rate. KKR bowlers maintained pressure, restricting MI to 147/8. Corbin Bosch provided some late hitting for Mumbai.

title: From strategic partnership to defence roadmap: Key outcomes of PM Modi–Meloni meeting
content: India and Italy have elevated their ties to a Special Strategic Partnership, fostering deeper cooperation in defence, technology, and trade. A defence roadmap for co-development and co-production of military platforms was agreed upon, alongside an ambitious €20 billion trade target by 2029. Discussions also covered global security, including West Asia and Ukraine, and maritime cooperation.

title: Aamir Khan says film failures push him into depression like losing a child
content: Aamir Khan has often spoken about the emotional investment he puts into his films, and the superstar has once again opened up about how deeply failures affect him. Known as Bollywood’s ‘Mr Perfectionist,’ Aamir revealed that he goes into a phase of emotional distress for months whenever one of his films fails at the box office. Interestingly, just a week ago, Aamir’s son Junaid Khan had also spoken about how the actor was struggling to deal with the poor performance of Ek Din. The film marked Sai Pallavi's Bollywood debut.

title: Horoscope: Daily astrological predictions for May 21
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 21, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: 24K love for gold: Why is India obsessed with the yellow metal?
content: India's enduring love for gold spans centuries, evolving from tradition to modern investment.  Women hold vast reserves, and the market is set for significant growth.  Digital gold and ETFs are gaining traction alongside traditional jewellery.  Gold remains a symbol of prosperity, security, and auspiciousness for Indians across generations.

title: From ‘Chicken’s Neck’ to Vande Mataram mandate: 11 key Suvendu govt decisions in 12 days
content: West Bengal's new BJP government is enacting swift policy changes, replacing TMC schemes with central programs like Ayushman Bharat and Annapurna Yojna. Mandatory 'Vande Mataram' in schools, revised OBC reservations, and increased age limits for government jobs are among the key decisions. The administration is also scrutinizing caste certificates and initiating corruption probes.

title: Mumbai Indians become second team in IPL history to ...
content: Mumbai Indians etched their name in IPL history for an unfortunate record, becoming the second team to field three different captains in three consecutive matches during the 2026 season. Injuries and player unavailability forced leadership changes, with Suryakumar Yadav, Jasprit Bumrah, and Hardik Pandya all taking the helm.

title: How CBSE topper prepared her notes to score 99.6%: Takeaway for parents & kids
content: CBSE class 12th topper Sonakshi Goyal's study notes are surprising students. She bound her handwritten notes directly with her NCERT books. This created an all-in-one revision system. Goyal's method kept all notes in one place for easier revision. She also revised topics daily and focused on NCERT. Practicing previous papers boosted her confidence. Conceptual clarity was key to her success.

title: IPL: What happens if rain washes out KKR vs MI at Eden Gardens?
content: Rain halted the IPL 2026 match between Kolkata Knight Riders and Mumbai Indians with MI at 57/4 in eight overs. If the game is washed out, both teams will share one point, badly hurting KKR’s playoff hopes as they can then reach only 14 points. KKR had made a strong start with the ball before rain interrupted play at Eden Gardens.

title: Trump Says Iran Talks In "Final Stages"
content: US President Donald Trump said Wednesday that talks with Tehran were "on the borderline" between a deal to end the Middle East war and a resumption of strikes on Iran.

title: 2 Minors Stabbed To Death In Delhi Over Old Rivalry: Cops
content: Two minors were allegedly stabbed to death after a violent clash between two groups over a past rivalry in northwest Delhi's Rohini area on Wednesday evening, police said.

title: Trump Calls Raul Castro Indictment "Very Big Moment", Rules Out Cuba Escalation
content: President Donald Trump hailed the US indictment of Cuba's former leader Raul Castro on Wednesday but said he did not see the need for escalation, amid speculation that Washington will try to topple the communist state.

title: US Charges Ex-Cuba President Raul Castro With Murder Over Downing Of Planes
content: The indictment was related to Castro's alleged role in the shootdown of two small planes operated by the exile group Brothers to the Rescue.

title: Drugs, Chinese Pistol Recovered Near LoC, Pakistan Link Suspected
content: Preliminary investigations suggest the consignment was thrown across the border from Pakistan by smugglers allegedly working in coordination with Pakistan's intelligence agency, the Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI).

title: Haryana: 20-Year-Old Gang Rape Survivor Dies By Suicide Over Blackmail
content: The family alleged that the accused gang-raped and filmed the crime. Following this, she was continuously blackmailed with threats of making the video viral.

title: "A Day Apart Feels Like 3 Autumns": Putin, Xi's Bonhomie In Beijing
content: Putin was received by Xi outside Beijing's opulent Great Hall of the People in much the same fashion as Trump last week, complete with chanting children and military fanfare.

title: Taxi, Auto Unions' 3-Day Strike In Delhi-NCR Begins Today
content: More than 68 transport unions under the banner of the All India Motor Transport Congress (AIMTC) and the United Front of All Transport Associations (UFTA) are expected to participate in the "chakka jam" protest.

title: Gita Gopinath Decodes Global Oil Shock, Backs PM's Fuel Cutback Advice
content: "If this conflict continues for a few more weeks, the right reaction is for there to be a cutback in usage of all these fields," because the petro prices are "expected to rise further," Gita Gopinath said

title: India And Italy Elevate Ties To 'Special Strategic Partnership'
content: At the heart of the economic agenda is an ambitious target: Expanding bilateral trade to 20 billion euros by 2029.

title: How Lashkar Terrorist Directed Pahalgam Terror Attack From Pakistan: NIA Chargesheet
content: Saifullah alias Sajid Jatt alias 'Langda', a terrorist of the Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) and its proxy The Resistance Front (TRF), operated from Pakistan's Lahore and gave instructions to the terrorists in Pahalgam, the NIA chargesheet said

title: J&K Congress Leaders Call For Rethink On Alliance With Omar Abdullah's Party
content: The rift came to light when 19 Congress district presidents in J&K urged the party high command to withdraw support from the National Conference.

title: 'Not Expecting More Company-Wide Layoffs This Year,' Meta CEO Tells Staff
content: He made the announcement on the same day the Facebook owner carried out a massive restructuring of the company, laying off 10 per cent of its workforce globally and transferring 7,000 other employees.

title: "Not In Line With Israel's Values": Netanyahu Rebukes Minister After Gaza Video
content: Netanyahu said that although Israel has every right to stop "provocative flotillas of Hamas terrorist supporters," the way National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir dealt with the activists was "not in line with Israel's values and norms."

title: Trump, Netanyahu Hold "Prolonged, Dramatic" Call Amid Iran Threat: Report
content: According to earlier Israeli media reports, the two leaders had also spoken on Sunday evening, when discussions reportedly centred on Trump's recent trip to China and his meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

title: Banks Can't Disable Mobile Phones Of Defaulting Borrowers: RBI Proposal
content: A bank will be allowed to restrict or disable functionalities of a defaulting borrower's mobile device, provided the device is financed by the lender.

title: PM Modi Receives Prestigious FAO Agricola Medal
content: PM Modi, who arrived in Italy on Tuesday night on the concluding leg of his five-nation tour, received the award from Dr Qu Dongyu

title: Sleeping Odisha Couple Set On Fire, Family Suspects Revenge Over Son's Affair
content: Bijay Behera and Aarti Behra were sleeping when a group barged into the house and set afire the cot they were sleeping on.

title: Bolster system in 20 days for secure test, govt tells NTA
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: Now 18, rape survivor wants to marry accused; HC grants him bail
content: Sanjeev Verma is a Senior Assistant Editor covering Himachal Pradesh for The Times of India. His journalism experience spans across multiple states, including Punjab and Haryana, in addition to covering the Punjab and Haryana High Court, after an initial stint in Delhi. He navigates the realms of politics, security, public policy, finance, industries and commerce, rural development, legal affairs, environment, defence services welfare, and NRI affairs, with a focus on investigative journalism. title: Bride subjected to sexual abuse, killed for dowry in Nagpur
content:
.

NAGPUR: A 19-year-old newlywed Nagpur woman, who was allegedly tortured for dowry and subjected to sexual harassment by her brother-in-law, was strangled to death last Friday by her in-laws, who claimed it as a fatal heart attack, reports Soumitra Bose. The plot unraveled after her body was exhumed last Sunday for autopsy, which revealed compression marks on the neck and signs of asphyxia, pointing to strangulation. The woman's parents had performed her last rites at a graveyard in Amravati city, where they resided. Two audio recordings of the woman's desperate calls to her mother were also clinching evidence that led to registration of a murder FIR and arrest of her husband and brother-in-law late Tuesday. Her father-in-law is yet to be taken into custody.

End of Article

title: Suvendu aide’s murder: Court frees man wrongly arrested by police
content: the court accepted the plea and ordered the release of Raj Singh, arrested in UP by an SIT of west Bengal police on may 11, five days after the murder . the central agency submitted in court that Raj, who was picked up along with Mayank Mishra and Vicky Maurya, had an alibi for the day of the killing . family Raj’s family has claimed he was in Uttar Pradesh when Rath was gunned down in Madhyamgram on may 6 .

title: India-Italy ties hit sweet spot: Deals on defence, critical minerals
content: it was the first bilateral visit by an Indian prime minister to the country in 26 years . there were 12 other outcomes from the visit, including an MoU to prevent tax crimes and money laundering . both leaders backed the IndiaMiddle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) project .
